data_quality: v0.1 best-effort population from cited public sources. Each entity's compat claims trace to source_ids on its attributes. Verification pass required before production launch; this dataset is sized to prove the engine end-to-end, not for general consumer trust.

sources.name: NREL PVWatts public solar resource API
sources.scope: Used for ground-truth solar yield modeling per location and for the assumptions baked into our charge-controller / array-sizing recommendations.

sources.name: Open Energy Information / OpenEI public datasets
sources.scope: Used for component classifications, irradiance reference tables, and battery-cycle-life baselines.

sources.name: Manufacturer spec sheets (public PDFs and product pages)
sources.scope: Used for electrical specs (Voc, Vmp, Imp, continuous/surge watts, MPPT range, capacity_ah, BMS limits, dimensions, weight). Vendors include Renogy, Victron, EcoFlow, Bluetti, Jackery, Goal Zero, Anker SOLIX, Battle Born, BougeRV, AIMS, Schneider, Growatt, EPEVER, Eco-Worthy.

sources.name: Solar enthusiast community references (DIY Solar Forum public threads, Will Prowse YouTube spec summaries, Mobile Solar Power public guides)
sources.scope: Used to cross-check vendor specs against measured real-world results, and to capture practical compatibility caveats (e.g. high-voltage panel arrays vs. 100V-class controllers, BMS reverse-polarity behavior, inverter idle draw).

attributes.note: Small-footprint 100W panel — common starter for 12V camper / RV builds. Voc fits any 100V-class MPPT in series-of-3 or fewer.
The original Renogy 100W — most-installed off-grid panel in the US. Pairs with any PWM or MPPT charge controller rated 10A+.
Single-panel 200W rigid — popular for medium-roof RVs that want one panel instead of two 100W. Needs ~30A MPPT at 12V.
24V-class panel; ground-mount and rooftop residential off-grid. Two in series exceed Victron 75/15 — needs 100V-class MPPT or higher.
High-voltage 400W panel; do NOT put two in series into a 100V controller — array Voc exceeds 99.4V at cold-temp coefficient. Use 150V+ MPPT.
Flexible panel for curved RV/boat roofs. Lower lifespan than rigid (~5 yr vs 25 yr) — buyer-beware for permanent installs.
Adhesive-mount flexible panel, common on van/boat curved roofs. Heat dissipation is poor on glued installs — derate ~15% in summer.
Large bifacial residential ground-mount panel. Backside gain ~10-20% over reflective surface. Requires 150V+ MPPT in any series config.
Budget bifacial alternative to Renogy 200W. Ground-mount with reflective surface to capture rear-side gain (5-15% real-world).
High-Voc 400W; needs 150V+ MPPT. Popular in DIY ground-mount arrays for cabins.
Foldable briefcase panel with stand and integrated PWM controller (bypassable). Targets portable power stations with DC8mm or MC4 inputs.
ETFE foldable panel, MC4 + DC + Anderson outputs. Pairs with Jackery / EcoFlow / Bluetti units that accept open-circuit voltage up to 22V.
Largest EcoFlow portable panel — pairs natively with DELTA Pro / Pro Ultra (Voc within DELTA Pro's 11-150V MPPT). Premium pricing vs DIY rigid.
Double-sided portable for DELTA / RIVER series. Bifacial gain only when stand keeps panel off the ground.
Compact 160W portable, MC4 output. Drop-in for any EcoFlow station; also works with Jackery via XT60-to-MC4 adapter.
Small briefcase panel, great for RIVER 2 / RIVER 2 Max. Single panel only — RIVER 2 Pro accepts 220W max input.
Heavy briefcase with kickstand. Native 8mm output for Yeti stations; MC4 dongle ships in box for third-party MPPTs.
100W rigid briefcase with kickstand. Premium price vs equivalent BougeRV; buy if already in Yeti ecosystem.
Ultra-light foldable cloth-frame panel. Pairs with Yeti 1000X/1500X via 8mm; output drops sharply when partially shaded — no bypass diodes per cell.
ETFE-laminated foldable, MC4 native. Three in parallel saturate AC200P / AC300 input.
High-voltage portable for AC300 / AC500 (12-150V MPPT). Voc rules out 24V-class controllers — meant for Bluetti's 145V MPPT.
Light foldable for EB3A/EB55/AC180. MC4 output works with any portable solar station.
Native DC8020 output for Explorer 1000 Pro / 2000 Pro / 3000 Pro. MC4 adapter sold separately for non-Jackery use.
Classic Jackery SolarSaga — MC4 + DC8020 outputs. Pairs with any Explorer station; many users daisy-chain 2-4 in parallel.
Smallest SolarSaga; matches Explorer 240 / 300 / 500 inputs. Voc within range for any portable power station.

entities.name: Anker SOLIX PS200 Portable Solar Panel

attributes.note: Anker 200W foldable, native XT60 + MC4. Drop-in for SOLIX C800 / C1000 / F2000.

entities.name: Anker SOLIX PS400 Portable Solar Panel

attributes.note: High-Voc portable for SOLIX F2000 / F3800 (11-60V MPPT). Will not work with 24V-class third-party MPPTs in series.
Budget alternative to Renogy 100W. Functionally equivalent on small 12V banks; quality control more variable per community reports.
Budget 195W panel, 12V-class. Often sold in 4-packs for ground-mount cabin arrays. Same dimensions as Renogy 200W.
Entry-level pure-sine inverter. Pairs with 100Ah-200Ah 12V LiFePO4 banks; 1000W continuous draws ~85A from battery — size cables and BMS accordingly.
2000W 12V draws ~170A continuous — needs 4/0 AWG cables and a 250A+ BMS battery (e.g. Battle Born 270Ah). Underpowered BMS will trip under surge.
3000W 24V — only ~125A draw at full load, much more practical wiring than 12V. Needs a matching 24V battery bank.

entities.name: Renogy 3500W Pure Sine Wave Inverter (48V Hybrid)

attributes.note: 48V hybrid inverter with built-in 80A MPPT and AC-pass-through. Single-unit off-grid for a small cabin; requires 48V battery bank.

entities.name: Renogy 700W Pure Sine Wave Compact Inverter

attributes.note: Compact 700W for under-seat van builds. Will not run microwaves or full-size induction; sized for laptops, fridges, lights.
Premium European-grade compact inverter. VE.Direct port lets you wire it into a Victron Cerbo GX for whole-system telemetry.
VE.Direct integration with Cerbo GX / GlobalLink. Premium price, low idle draw (~5W) — better for always-on liveaboard than budget inverters.
Combined inverter + 120A charger + AC pass-through. Industry-standard for liveaboard boats and high-end RV builds. VE.Bus telemetry.
48V whole-home off-grid backbone. Stackable up to 3-phase or split-phase 240V via paralleling. Pairs with any 48V LFP bank with closed-loop CAN BMS.

entities.name: AIMS Power 2000W Pure Sine Wave Inverter (12V)

attributes.note: Mid-tier US-built 2000W inverter. Optional remote-on/off panel; AIMS warranty handled in-house, faster RMA than Chinese-import alts.

entities.name: AIMS Power 5000W Pure Sine Wave Inverter (24V)

attributes.note: Large 24V inverter for cabin / shop loads. Needs 24V LFP bank with 250A+ BMS — full surge can briefly draw 400A.

entities.name: AIMS Power 6000W Pure Sine Wave Inverter (48V Split-Phase)

attributes.note: Split-phase 120/240V — runs well pumps, dryers, mini-splits. Standalone (no built-in MPPT); pair with separate 60-100A controller.
All-in-one with built-in 80A MPPT and AC charger. Popular budget cabin off-grid choice; firmware quirks with non-Growatt 24V LFP banks reported.
48V single-phase 5kW with 100A MPPT + 60A AC charger. Stackable up to 6 units. The DIY Solar Forum's most-recommended budget 48V hybrid.
Split-phase 120/240V hybrid; dual MPPT inputs, 120A total. Needs 48V LFP with closed-loop comms for safe high-current charging.

entities.name: Schneider Electric Conext XW Pro 6848 Inverter/Charger

attributes.note: Top-tier whole-home 48V split-phase inverter/charger. Stack up to 4 in parallel for 27.2 kW continuous. Industrial reliability; install requires Schneider gateway.
LP / gasoline backup generator that auto-starts to recharge DELTA Pro / Pro Ultra when bank state-of-charge is low. Not a standalone solar inverter.

entities.name: Bluetti EP500 Pro All-in-One Inverter Module

attributes.note: EP500 Pro is technically a power station, but it operates as a 3kW 48V inverter against a built-in 5.1 kWh LFP bank. Treated as an inverter for sizing logic.
Budget 2000W; community reports surge handling marginal. Acceptable for resistive loads; do not pair with hard-start motors / well pumps.

entities.name: Krieger KR1100 1100W Pure Sine Wave Inverter

attributes.note: Mid-budget mobile inverter. Built-in remote, ANL fuses included. Pairs with 100Ah-200Ah 12V banks for car-camp / van loads.

entities.name: Samlex PST-3000-12 Pure Sine Wave Inverter

attributes.note: Industrial-grade 12V 3kW. Conservative continuous rating (true 3000W vs marketing-3000W from cheap brands). Marine-grade build.
Cheaper modified-sine variant — DO NOT use with sensitive electronics, motors, CPAPs, or LED displays. Resistive loads (heaters, pumps) only.
Pure inverter (no charger). Native Bluetooth + VE.Direct. Use when MultiPlus charger feature is unwanted (e.g. fully-DC-coupled solar build).

entities.name: AIMS Power 1200W Pure Sine Wave Inverter (12V)

attributes.note: Mid-size 1200W with hardwired AC outputs (no NEMA outlets). Used in van/RV builds where the inverter feeds a 30A AC sub-panel.
Compact 15A MPPT with built-in Bluetooth + VictronConnect. Tops out at 200W on 12V or 400W on 24V. Single 100W panel: ideal pair.
20A controller, 100V PV. Two 200W panels in series fit; 12V→290W max, 24V→580W. The most common 'second 100W panel + small bank' MPPT.
30A / 100V. 12V→440W max, 24V→880W. Sweet spot for ~400W rooftop array on 12V camper.
50A / 100V. 12V→700W, 24V→1400W. Pair with 600-800W rooftop arrays at 12V.
35A / 150V. Required when running residential 60-cell or 72-cell panels in series. 12V→500W, 48V→2000W.
85A / 150V — large off-grid cabin scale. 48V→4900W array. Standard for 5-10 kWh battery banks with serious solar.
100A / 250V — flagship Victron MPPT. 48V→5800W. Can string 4-5 residential panels in series; ideal for ground-mount cabin arrays.
Budget 30A MPPT. Bluetooth requires optional BT-1/BT-2 dongle (extra ~$40). Direct competitor to Victron 100/30 at half the price.
40A / 100V; pair with up to 520W on 12V or 1040W on 24V. BT-1 dongle for app monitoring sold separately.
60A 150V — handles serial residential panels (Voc ~50V each, 3-in-series safe). Common cabin off-grid choice.
100A / 200V — large array support. 48V→5300W. Direct alternative to Victron 250/100 at lower price; no native Bluetooth.
Cheap PWM-only controller. ~70-80% efficiency vs MPPT's 95%+; only acceptable for tiny systems where panel Vmp ≈ battery voltage.
Industry-favorite 40A budget MPPT. Pairs with optional MT-50 display or eBox-WiFi for remote monitoring.
60A controller. Handles 24V or 48V banks; native RS485 / MODBUS for HA / Node-RED integration.
Tiny 10A MPPT — 100W panel + 12V battery sweet spot. No Bluetooth; SmartSolar 75/15 is $40 more and adds it. Most users should pay the extra.
The North American gold-standard 12V LFP. 100A continuous BMS limit — 1200W inverter max on a single battery. US support, 10-yr warranty.
High-output 270Ah single-battery bank for full-time RV / liveaboard. 200A BMS supports 2000W continuous inverter draw. Heated optional.
GC2 form-factor (golf-cart) — drop-in for trolling motors / heritage RV battery boxes. Heated to allow charging in sub-freezing temps.
Mid-tier 100Ah with Bluetooth-monitored BMS. Cells balance via app. Solid budget Battle Born alternative; warranty terms shorter.
200A BMS — 2000W inverter compatible. Communicates state-of-charge via Renogy ONE / DC Home app. Heating pad optional add-on.
High-capacity 12V LFP. Fewer 12V batteries to wire vs multiple 100Ah; fits standard Group 8D battery box.
Single-unit 24V bank. Avoids the need to series two 12V batteries (which restricts BMS coordination). 2.4 kWh per battery.
Rack-mount 48V battery; closed-loop CAN/RS485 comms with Growatt / EG4 / Sol-Ark 48V hybrid inverters. 2.5 kWh; stack 4-16 in parallel.

entities.name: EcoFlow DELTA Pro Extra Battery 3.6 kWh

attributes.note: DELTA Pro slave-pack only — does not work as standalone battery. Connects via the proprietary infinity-port cable.

entities.name: EcoFlow DELTA 2 Max Extra Battery 2.0 kWh

attributes.note: Slave-pack for DELTA 2 Max only. Capacity stacks 2x or 4x; not usable as a standalone battery.

entities.name: Goal Zero Yeti Tank Pro 4000 Expansion

attributes.note: Slave-pack for Yeti Pro 4000. 4 kWh add. Premium price; only justified inside Goal Zero ecosystem with existing Yeti Pro 4000.
Expansion for AC200P / AC200MAX. Has its own AC/DC outputs — usable as a small standalone power station, unlike most expansion packs.
48V slave-pack for AC300 / AC500. Standalone usable as a 12V/24V output bank via bypass — see Bluetti spec sheet.
Successor to B300, optimized for AC500 / Apex 300 platforms. LFP chemistry, faster cell-balancing firmware than B300.
US-warrantied 100Ah 12V LFP. No Bluetooth; pairs with any standard 12V system. Mid-tier price/quality balance.
48V single-unit 5.1 kWh bank. RS485 closed-loop comms with AIMS or Schneider inverters. Wall-mount or rack.
Aggressive-priced 300Ah from LiTime. Real-world reviews confirm rated capacity. 200A BMS = 2000W inverter compatible.
Bluetooth-enabled 200Ah at half the price of Renogy 200Ah Smart. Will Prowse-recommended budget pick.
Cheapest reputable 100Ah LFP. No frills, no Bluetooth, but UL-listed and tested by community reviewers. Drop-in for any 12V LFP role.
Single-unit 24V 100Ah — 2.56 kWh. Avoids series-stacking 12V cells. Pairs with 24V Renogy / Victron / AIMS inverters.
5.1 kWh rack-mount with closed-loop CAN/RS485 to Growatt / EG4 / Sol-Ark / Schneider. Stacks up to 16 in parallel.
Premium-build 5 kWh rack battery. Closed-loop CAN to most major hybrids. Heating pad standard, 6000-cycle rating.
Industry-standard rack LFP. Closed-loop CAN with EG4 / Sol-Ark / Growatt. UL-certified, sub-$0.30/Wh — current best-value rack battery.
Lead-acid AGM. ~50% usable capacity (discharge to 50% only); shorter lifespan than LFP. Cheap upfront but worse $/kWh-cycle.
GC3 form factor (industrial battery cabinet). Bluetooth state-of-charge, integrated heating, 250A BMS = 2500W inverter capable.
Top-tier prismatic-cell LFP with 400A BMS, supports 4000W inverters on a single battery. Premium pricing — pro/RV liveaboard tier.
Mid-shelf 150Ah w/ self-heating, Bluetooth, and rust-resistant case. Pairs with Magnum / Schneider / Outback inverters in marine / cabin builds.
Two 6V FLA batteries in series = 12V 225Ah. Cheapest $/Wh upfront, but requires monthly water-topping and venting. Cabin-only.
Heritage off-grid choice. 430Ah at 12V (only 50% usable) = ~2.6 kWh practical. Vented battery box required. Pre-LFP-era technology.
Smallest LFP Jackery. CPAP / laptop tier. 100W solar input — pair with one SolarSaga 100W.
Older NMC chemistry — lower cycle life than newer LFP siblings. Discontinued in some regions; clearance pricing common.
LFP refresh of the original 1000. 4000-cycle rating. Solid camp/blackout middle-of-the-road station.
High solar input (800W) but legacy NMC chemistry — Jackery is migrating to LFP, this Pro line is the prior generation.
LFP 2 kWh expandable to 24 kWh via add-on packs. Supports 240V output when paired with a second unit. Whole-home backup tier.
3 kWh standalone, NMC chemistry. Heavy at 64 lb — no longer the recommended Jackery flagship; the 2000 Plus / 5000 Plus are LFP.
LFP 1 kWh, expandable to 3 kWh via DELTA 2 / Max extra batteries. X-Boost mode pushes 2200W resistive loads.
2 kWh expandable to 6 kWh via two B2-MAX slave packs. 1000W MPPT. Whole-evening backup tier; not whole-home.
3.6 kWh standalone, expandable to 25 kWh w/ 2x extras + 2x Smart Generators. AC-coupled to home panel via Smart Home Panel 2.
Per-inverter unit — 7.2 kW output, 6 kWh per battery slot, expandable to 90 kWh. True split-phase 240V; whole-home backup tier.
Smallest LFP RIVER. CPAP overnight + small fridge. 1-hour AC fast-charge.
LFP 768 Wh, 4-outlet station. Sweet spot for weekend camp + driveway power outage.
LFP 1.15 kWh, fast 1.5-hr AC charge. Wireless pad on top. Mid-tier camp/backup.
2 kWh LFP expandable to 8 kWh via B230/B300 packs. 900W MPPT. NEMA 14-50 RV-compatible output port.
AC300 inverter requires at least one B300 battery; expandable to 24 kWh + dual-unit split-phase 240V via P030A fusion box.
5 kW continuous; expandable to 18.4 kWh and split-phase 240V via two-unit pairing. Whole-home short-duration backup.
LFP successor to original Yeti 1000. App-monitored, 600W solar input. Premium price vs DELTA 2 / AC180 for similar specs.
NMC chemistry — fewer cycles than LFP rivals. Hardwire-ready Yeti Home Integration Kit; Goal Zero ecosystem premium.
LFP 4 kWh, 3 kW MPPT. Expand with Tank Pro 4000 packs. Goal Zero's flagship; Anderson + XT60 inputs accept any third-party MC4 panel.
Mid-size LFP station, 6-outlet. Surge mode handles 2400W resistive. Strong $/Wh competitor to DELTA 2 and AC180.
LFP 2 kWh; expandable to 4 kWh via BP2000 add-on. RV-ready NEMA TT-30 output. Solid 2 kWh tier.
True split-phase 120V/240V output, 6 kW continuous, expandable to 53 kWh. Most cost-effective whole-home backup station as of 2026.

entities.name: Renogy Z-Bracket Solar Panel Mount Kit

attributes.note: Standard rooftop fixed mount — bolts to RV roof / shed roof. One kit (4 brackets) per panel. Most-installed mount type in 12V off-grid.
Adjustable 0/15/30/45-deg tilt. Roof or ground. Improves winter yield 20-40% at high latitudes vs flat-mount.

entities.name: Renogy Solar Panel Ground Mount System (3-Panel)

attributes.note: 3-panel adjustable-tilt ground mount. Concrete-anchor or earth-screw. Sized for 60/72-cell residential panels.
Top-of-pole mount; common for cabin water-pump systems. Fits 4-5 in pole, single panel up to 200W.

entities.name: Renogy ABS Corner Brackets (Set of 4) — RV Adhesive

attributes.note: No-drill RV-roof adhesive corners. Sealant + Eternabond is mandatory; raw-adhesive installs fail in 1-2 summers.
Cheaper Z-bracket alternative to Renogy. Same form factor; thinner aluminum stock — fine for sub-50-mph wind areas.
Budget tilt bracket. Holds up to 100W panel; not rated for 200W rigid in high-wind areas.
4-panel ground rack. Pre-drilled for 60/72-cell panels; concrete-anchor required. Lower steel wall thickness than IronRidge XR-1000.

entities.name: Goal Zero Boulder Hardware Mounting Kit

attributes.note: Rooftop bolt-down kit for Goal Zero Boulder rigid panels. Bracket length matches Boulder 100/200 frame; doesn't fit standard 100W.

entities.name: MaxxAir RV Solar Panel Tilt Arms (Pair)

attributes.note: RV-specific roof-tilt arms — paired with Z-brackets. Tilts panel to 30/45/60 deg from rear. Improves winter parked-camper yield.

entities.name: AM Solar Tilting Roof Mount Kit (Single Panel)

attributes.note: Premium full-time RV/boat tilting mount. Stainless hardware. Often paired with VHB tape + screws on aluminum-skin RVs.
Industry-standard residential rail. 90 mph wind / 60 psf snow rated. Used on grid-tied installs and 48V cabin systems.
Compresion lugs vs split-bolts speed install vs IronRidge. Same wind ratings. UL 2703 listed.
Helical earth screws as a no-concrete alternative under 4-panel ground mounts. Avoid in soft / rocky soil.

entities.name: AM Solar Side-of-Pole Mount Single Panel

attributes.note: Side-of-pole mount for cabin water/lighting setups. Mounts to 2-3 in pipe. Tighter form factor than top-of-pole.
Standard PV connector. Crimp tool sold separately; do NOT use mismatched MC4 brand pairs (a known fire-risk per NEC).

entities.name: Renogy 10 AWG MC4-to-MC4 Solar Cable Pair (20 ft)

attributes.note: 10 AWG handles up to ~30A safely; sized for a single 200W panel run. Use 8 AWG for parallel pairs of 200W panels.

entities.name: Renogy 8 AWG MC4 Solar Extension Pair (15 ft)

attributes.note: 8 AWG good for ~50A. Sized for 2x 200W parallel runs to a 30A MPPT.

entities.name: Renogy 6 AWG MC4 Solar Extension Pair (25 ft)

attributes.note: 6 AWG handles ~75A; sized for 4x 200W parallel array or long ground-mount-to-shed runs.
2-to-1 parallel combiner. Combines two panels' outputs into one MC4 input. Limit total current to controller's input rating.
4-to-1 parallel combiner. Use with 4-panel arrays going into a 60A MPPT controller. Fuse each branch separately per NEC.
Inline branch fuse. Required by NEC when paralleling 3+ strings; protects each panel's wiring from reverse-current.
15A inline fuse for individual residential-panel branches with Imp ~10A.

entities.name: Renogy ANL Fuse Kit 200A (Battery to Inverter)

attributes.note: 200A ANL fuse + holder. Required between LFP battery + and any 2000W+ inverter on 12V. Sizing: inverter_W / 12V × 1.25.

entities.name: Renogy 4 AWG Battery-to-Inverter Cable Pair (3 ft)

attributes.note: 4 AWG good for ~100A continuous; pair with 12V 1000W inverter. For 2000W+ step up to 2/0 or 4/0 AWG.

constraints.rationale: An inverter's DC input voltage is fixed (12 / 24 / 48). A 12V battery bank cannot drive a 24V or 48V inverter (under-voltage cutoff), and a 48V bank into a 12V inverter destroys the inverter on first connection. Bank nominal voltage must match input_voltage_dc exactly.

Cold-weather Voc rises ~10-25% above STC nameplate. If the array's series Voc at the coldest expected temperature exceeds the controller's max PV voltage, the controller fries. Always size with cold-temp coefficient × series count × Voc, not Vmp.

Charge controllers cap output current. P_max = max_current_amps × battery_nominal_voltage. Exceeding this either clips harvest (wasted panels) or, on cheap controllers, burns out the output stage. ~20-30% array oversizing is acceptable on MPPT for cloudy-day boost; beyond that, upsize the controller.

LFP batteries need 14.2-14.6V bulk on a 12V bank (no temperature compensation, no equalization). Lead-acid (FLA/AGM) needs 14.4-14.8V bulk plus periodic equalization at 15.5V. A controller without an LFP profile will under-charge LFP, and an LFP profile applied to FLA causes sulfation. Confirm chemistry-specific profile before pairing.

Continuous inverter draw (W ÷ V) cannot exceed the battery's BMS continuous current rating, or the BMS shuts the bank off mid-load (or, on cheap BMSes, fails). Example: a 2000W 12V inverter draws ~170A; a 100A-BMS battery cannot supply it without tripping. Match BMS amps to inverter peak draw.

All-in-one solar generators have a fixed MPPT input voltage range. EcoFlow DELTA Pro accepts 11-150V; Jackery Explorer 1000 v2 only 12-50V. A high-Voc panel (e.g. 60-cell residential, Voc ~50V) will damage a small RIVER-class station. Match panel Voc to station MPPT range.

Battery-to-inverter cable gauge must safely carry continuous current with 25% margin per NEC. 12V 2000W draws ~170A (need 4/0 AWG); 24V 2000W draws ~85A (4 AWG suffices); 48V 2000W draws ~42A (8 AWG). Undersized cables overheat under load; correctly sized cables also drop less voltage at the inverter.

then.mount.attributes.mount_type: in [z_bracket, tilt_adjustable, ground_mount, pole_mount, rooftop_rail, rv_roof_tilt, rooftop_mount, adhesive_corner]

constraints.rationale: Rigid framed panels need framed-panel hardware (Z-brackets, tilt arms, rails). Flexible panels are adhesive-mounted and have no frame for bolt-on hardware. Portable folding panels come with their own kickstand and need no mount. Selecting a mismatched mount voids the install.

goals.prompt: Meet the user's stated daily kWh demand at the user's location with margin for cloudy days.
goals.target: shortfall_days_per_year given NREL PVWatts irradiance × array_watts × derate(0.77)

goals.prompt: Across all selected components, minimize total cost per usable kWh of daily harvest.
When two batteries are equally compatible, prefer LiFePO4 — higher cycle life and 80% usable capacity vs. 50% on lead-acid wins on $/kWh-cycle.
Prefer pure-sine inverters; modified-sine units fail with motors, CPAPs, and modern electronics.
